report_name: Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens' Intensifies Espionage with New RATs Targeting US, Israel, and UAE

threat_actors.name: Screening Serpens
threat_actors.description: An Iran-nexus advanced persistent threat (APT) group conducting cyber-espionage campaigns against technology professionals in the U.S., Israel, and the UAE. Deployed new malware families MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 and used AppDomainManager hijacking for persistence.
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering, theft of intellectual property, and gaining access to strategic partner networks.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Used as the initial access vector by sending highly targeted emails with malicious links, such as a spoofed recruitment URL, to technology professionals.

attack_patterns.name: Application Shimming
attack_patterns.description: Used for persistence via AppDomainManager hijacking. The group created a malicious .NET assembly and set COMPLUS environment variables to force any .NET application to load their malicious code upon startup.

attack_patterns.name: Visual Basic
attack_patterns.description: VBScript was used by the malware loaders to execute the next stage of the infection chain.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
attack_patterns.description: The group likely used Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) for execution, as indicated by the hunting hint to monitor for 'wmic.exe process call create'.

attack_patterns.name: MSBuild
attack_patterns.description: The group likely abused the Microsoft Build Engine (MSBuild.exe) to execute malicious code embedded in project files, bypassing application whitelisting.

malware.name: MiniUpdate
malware.description: A new Remote Access Trojan (RAT) family deployed by Screening Serpens in 2026 espionage campaigns. It is used for intelligence gathering and maintaining access to compromised systems.

malware.name: MiniJunk V2
malware.description: A new Remote Access Trojan (RAT) family deployed by Screening Serpens alongside MiniUpdate in its 2026 espionage campaigns.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: The Windows Management Instrumentation Command-line utility (wmic.exe) was likely used by Screening Serpens for execution and lateral movement.

tools.name: MSBuild
tools.description: The Microsoft Build Engine (MSBuild.exe) was likely abused by Screening Serpens to execute malicious code from project files to bypass application whitelisting.

identities.name: Technology Sector Professionals
identities.description: Professionals in the technology sectors of the U.S., Israel, and the UAE targeted by Screening Serpens for intelligence gathering and intellectual property theft.

indicators.name: Screening Serpens AppDomainManager Hijacking Persistence
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Screening Serpens AppDomainManager Hijacking Persistence
id: 2a9b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation or modification of the COMPLUS_Version environment variable, a technique used by Screening Serpens for AppDomainManager hijacking persistence.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/screening-serpens-apt-espionage/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1546.011
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|contains: '\Environment\COMPLUS_Version'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the COMPLUS_Version environment variable, a strong indicator of the AppDomainManager hijacking technique used by Screening Serpens for persistence.

indicators.name: Screening Serpens WMIC Process Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Screening Serpens WMIC Process Creation
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-1b0a-987654321fed
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'wmic.exe process call create', a technique used for execution and lateral movement by groups like Screening Serpens.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/screening-serpens-apt-espionage/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1047
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'process call create'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'wmic.exe' to launch new processes, a common execution technique used by Screening Serpens.

indicators.name: Screening Serpens MSBuild Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Screening Serpens MSBuild Execution
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of MSBuild.exe, which can be abused by threat actors like Screening Serpens to execute malicious code and bypass application whitelisting.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/screening-serpens-apt-espionage/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1127.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\MSBuild.exe'
  filter_legit:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\devenv.exe'
      - '\VsBuild.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects the execution of MSBuild.exe, which Screening Serpens may abuse to run malicious code embedded in project files.
